session: akiendelllinux_cc_0 — a sprint session (Akien: 'sprint until everything that can be done is, even if it results in some rework... it's self correcting'). Reworked the code to the heartbeat+callback+bus model that converged in the prior session's design conversation. Supersedes the next_direction of 2026-07-18-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus (that slate's design capture still stands; this slate records the code catching up to it).
author: CC
in_flight: REWORK SHIPPED and PROVEN — the code caught up to the reshaped runtime model (authoritative model: CairnCommons/intentions/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md, whose '— DONE' section names the commits). Each piece proven bare AND under the tester, committed separately, both repos pushed. Full-suite: 11 proofs green; the composition trap now bites on 6 real device/shim subclasses (verified by importing all + running the sweep). What shipped, in dependency order: (1) THE BUS (cairn d27ce1d, cairn/bus/) — the second substrate, the sole owner-gated path for ALL inter-device comms (symmetric with db_domain=sole state path); durable transit rides db_domain (owner 'bus'); per-device channels announce/personal (records of truth) + info/debug (diagnostic); records REFUSE to collapse, diagnostic VIEWs may (Law 7); every envelope carries why+causality (Law 5). (2) THE CALLBACK PRIMITIVE + BaseShim FIRING (cairn ae2d372) — cairn/base/callback.py: an immutable 'poke X when trigger true', trigger = ANY predicate (now,ctx)->bool, NOT a named kind (the enum deleted). cairn/base/shim.py: on_pulse fires due callbacks onto the bus (batch-safe, CP2/Law 7), deliver/_start_device wake the device on demand. The long-deferred one-loop primitive RESOLVED: the heartbeat IS the one loop. (3) GROUND_LOOP -> THE HEARTBEAT (cairn a535de0) — beat(now,ctx) pulses subscribed shims; run_driver + the method-registry + collect fixtures + the executor proof RETIRED with the goof. (4) SYSTEM_RACKMOUNT -> THE SYSTEM DEVICE (cairn faafb70) — owns host-resource predicates; advertise->subscribe->poke; evaluates LOCALLY so the host reading never crosses the bus (Law 6, asserted in the capstone proof, which composes every piece end-to-end through the real bus); SchedulerService + the interval/date/quantity/state enum DELETED. (5) MAP.md spine corrected (cairn 7d546d8). All four component intentions rewritten from 'design ahead of code' to 'IMPLEMENTED and PROVEN'; the bus got a fresh charter.
next_direction: THE SPINE RESUMES (MAP.md build order): inference domain -> web server -> librarian-as-chatbot -> graph trees. The 'rack' step is DONE/subsumed (the chassis = the bus + the shims, both built; there is no separate rack device). The inference domain is the next real stone: the inference host reached ONLY through inference_domain (a CLAUDE.md physics IOU still open — it retires when the tester's import-scan tooth makes 'sole path' physics). Before or alongside: the emit-chokepoint (the base-class spine step that ENFORCES the node state-machine table, Law 4) — it is the gate the mutable TICKET species and the retired proven-space method-registry both wait on (both RETURN when it lands; the registry's design is proven in git at 584aa74, so re-instate not re-derive, Law 1). Also named by Akien for 'tomorrow' (NOT started, just noted): a new daily REPORT of intentions designed vs intentions implemented — a review surface for the from-intention method. And the standing 'thing-for-akien': a Cairn-native sudo-relay so Akien only starts+approves and CC self-serves root (the UU-tree daemon today is a stopgap).
open_threads: FILED EDGES on the shipped pieces (shape proven, growth deferred against real need — NOT faked): (bus) MCP wire-adapter at the edge; per-device-OWNED channels (today the bus owns one transit table + attributes senders); diagnostic retention/expiry; wiring Akien's feeds (the web-server stone). (shim) each device its own OS PROCESS — start-on-demand shape is proven, real separate-process spawn + file-based subscription discovery grow when devices are separate processes. (system device) only cpu_threshold advertised (load-average sampler) — memory/disk predicates are the same shape, mount when a consumer asks; subscriptions are LEVEL-triggered (edge-triggering + unsubscribe deferred); advertise/subscribe as bus messages (the poke already is). All in the component charters + the design doc's '— DONE' section.

THE MUTABLE TICKET SPECIES stays DISTINCT and DEFERRED (by design, not the goof). The callback/ticket boundary is clean in code now (callback = immutable worker, class-space; ticket = mutable workflow node, instance-space; LEARNING is a state, a callback is the worker it sets). The ticket state machine + the emit-chokepoint that enforces it are still unbuilt — CairnCommons/tickets/state-machine-physics.json (updated this session with the rework note: the driver-executor + scheduler entries retired; the proven-space registry returns with the emit-chokepoint).

THE META-LESSON Akien named, worth keeping: intention-first is 'self correcting' — the wrong code is CHEAP because the intention is where you find out; the model (drawn slowly with Akien) is the asset, the code is downstream. Law 5 (intent+impl share an address) is the mechanism: co-located why-vs-code is the diff surface that made the mismatch visible; the three reify-vs-flow errors were caught at n=1. And the flowed-apart shapes were SIMPLER than the frozen ones (three mechanisms -> two substrates; predicate < enum+dispatch) — the flow bias paid out as both more-correct AND less-code. Memory [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] updated with the confirmation.

CONVENTION for THIS session's commits (a change from the carried convention): the commit trailer used the full Co-Authored-By + Claude-Session lines from the harness environment block (not the plain trailer the prior slate carried). If Akien wants the plain trailer, note it — I followed the environment's stated format. GitHub hosting-only; proofs run bare (python3, sys.path parents[N]); commit often, push at 'smells like done'.

STANDING IOUs (unchanged): the three CLAUDE.md physics rules (durable state via db_domain / inference host via inference_domain / port 5432 via db_domain) retire fully only when the tester IMPORT-SCAN tooth makes 'sole path' physics — the bus adds a fourth of the same shape ('inter-device comms via the bus only') that the same tooth will eventually enforce. node-state vocab (PROVED/LEARNING) still not wired into device.state(). The 'every charter answers how-it-learns' rule (Law 8 non-hollow) — all shipped charters carry how_it_learns.

MEMORY refreshed this session (~/.claude, not in git): next-direction-session-continuity.md (new LATEST part-2 = the rework shipped, with commits + the meta-lesson), [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] (+ the built-cleanly confirmation), [[rack-vs-system-rackmount-vs-scheduler]] (+ SHIPPED note superseding the stale 'Built 2026-07-18 SchedulerService' lines). MEMORY.md index current.
VERIFY-BEFORE-ACTING (Law 3): this slate names commits + files as pointers, not proofs. Next session, a `git log --oneline` and a full-suite proof run confirm the territory before building on it.
